description: Generate production-ready static ads from chat — brief, Gemini-refined prompt, NB2 image, and ad name — for any product in your catalog.

post-install.name: Create image server app
post-install.description: Registers the nb-image-gen app container. Source files are already in place from the install step.

post-install.name: Build image server
post-install.description: Installs dependencies and compiles server + frontend

post-install.name: Verify image server
post-install.description: Confirms the built bundle is ready

post-install.name: Surface app URL
post-install.description: Captures the public host for the image CDN — save this as APP_HOST, you will need it during setup

post-install.name: Personalize for this sandbox
post-install.description: Walks through API keys, workspace ID, brand config, reference image upload, and product spec generation

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.

customize.description: Your brand's full display name, e.g. Acme Co
One or two-letter brand code used in ad names, e.g. A
Lowercase kebab-case brand name used in file paths, e.g. acme-co
Motion workspace ID — detected automatically during setup
Public host for the nb-image-gen app — surfaced after app build

changelog.notes: Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Initial release. End-to-end chat-native static ad generation: Gemini brief refinement, NB2 image generation, automated product spec extraction from uploaded images, ad naming, and learning log entries. Includes four-step onboarding: API keys, workspace ID auto-detection, brand config, and guided per-product setup.
